text: The world here is polished, orderly, and oddly silent—modern glass and the promise of technology hum just beneath the surface. As I look into the empty space behind the transparent walls, it feels like waiting at the threshold of creation or discovery. I sense potential energy—not anxious, but thoughtful. Every reflection and line draws me into speculation about purpose, about the invisible workings of people and systems alike. I feel curious and quietly reflective, wondering what unseen stories pass through these clean halls.
